requirements.title: Mutex Root Structure
requirements.description: The mutex root shall embed a module root with an API vtable pointer.
requirements.rationale: An abstract mutex interface decouples synchronization logic from platform-specific locking primitives (RTOS mutexes, spinlocks, etc.), enabling portability and testability.

requirements.title: TryLock Operation
requirements.description: The mutex API vtable shall define a TryLock operation that attempts to acquire the lock and returns immediately with a boolean result.
requirements.rationale: TryLock enables non-blocking lock acquisition, which is essential for real-time tasks that cannot afford to block indefinitely on a contended resource.

requirements.title: Lock Operation
requirements.description: The mutex API vtable shall define a Lock operation that blocks until the lock is acquired.
requirements.rationale: Blocking Lock provides the standard synchronization primitive for tasks that can afford to wait, complementing the non-blocking TryLock.

requirements.title: Free Operation
requirements.description: The mutex API vtable shall define a Free operation that releases the lock.
requirements.rationale: Free releases the lock, enabling other tasks to acquire it; without Free the mutex would be permanently held.

requirements.title: Mutex Vtable Validation
requirements.description: The mutex module shall verify that its API vtable and all function pointers within it are non-NULL.
requirements.rationale: Validates the vtable at runtime to ensure memory safety before dispatching any mutex operation through function pointers.
